title: Robot Music Video Director
description: The player directs a music video by positioning an Xbot robot performer on a stage with a BoomBox, then placing and aiming an AntiqueCamera to capture the perfect shot. Players must adjust camera angle, zoom, stage lighting color, robot pose trigger, boombox volume visualizer intensity, and confirm the final take — all validated against target parameters for a high director score.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a 'Robot Music Video Director' game. Load BoomBox.glb (scale to ~50x, place center-stage on a circular platform), Xbot.glb (scale to ~1.5, standing behind the boombox), and AntiqueCamera.glb (scale to ~30x, placed on a dolly track arc in front of the stage). Scene setup: a dark studio environment with a ground plane, subtle fog, and ambient light (intensity 0.3). Add a colored SpotLight pointing at the stage. The user's perspective is a free OrbitControls overview camera looking at the whole set. Implement a right-side HTML/CSS control panel with these 6 validated interactions: (1) A slider 'Camera Dolly Position' (0-360 degrees) that orbits the AntiqueCamera around the stage on a radius-5 arc — must be set between 30-60 degrees to pass. (2) A slider 'Camera Tilt' (-45 to 45 degrees) rotating the AntiqueCamera's X-axis — must be between -10 and 5 degrees. (3) A color picker 'Stage Light Color' that changes the SpotLight color — must be set to a hue in the purple range (H:260-300 in HSL). (4) A slider 'Boombox Pulse Intensity' (0-10) that scales the BoomBox with a sine-wave pulsing animation at that amplitude factor — must be between 6 and 8. (5) A button 'Trigger Robot Pose' that cycles the Xbot through 3 preset rotation poses (arms-up via rotating upper body parts) — must be on pose index 2. (6) A 'Confirm Take' button that validates ALL parameters, shows a floating 3D Text score overlay (green 'PERFECT TAKE' if all pass, red 'RESHOOT' with failing parameter names otherwise). Add a small picture-in-picture preview div (top-left, 300x200) rendering from the AntiqueCamera's perspective using a second WebGLRenderer or viewport so the user sees what the antique camera sees. Use AnimationMixer for any Xbot animations found in the GLB. Add post-processing bloom (UnrealBloomPass) on the main view for cinematic feel.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  cameraDollyAngle: number,         // angle in degrees (0-360) of AntiqueCamera position on arc around stage
  cameraTiltAngle: number,         // tilt angle in degrees (-45 to 45) of AntiqueCamera X-axis rotation
  stageLightHue: number,         // hue value (0-360) of the SpotLight color illuminating the stage
  boomboxPulseIntensity: number,         // intensity value (0-10) controlling BoomBox scale pulsing amplitude
  robotPoseIndex: number,         // integer index (0-2) selecting which animation pose the Xbot is performing
  cameraZoomLevel: number,         // zoom factor (0.5-2.0) controlling AntiqueCamera lens field of view
  recordingActive: boolean,         // whether the music video recording has been confirmed and started
  validCameraDolly: boolean,         // true if cameraDollyAngle is between 30-60 degrees
  validCameraTilt: boolean,         // true if cameraTiltAngle is between -10 and 5 degrees
  validStageLightColor: boolean,         // true if stageLightHue is between 260-300 (purple range)
  setupComplete: boolean,         // true when all positioning and settings have been validated
}
